headline: Cleveland Fed: AI may fuel inflation, rate hikes possible
detail: Fed's Hammack explicitly linking AI-driven demand to inflation risk puts rate hikes back on the table. Higher rates compress tech and growth stock valuations directly - your portfolio is ~90% concentrated there. This also pressures leveraged plays like SOXL and margin positions across the board.
rec: Consider trimming SOXL on any intraday bounce - scanner shows no fresh setup there, and 3x leverage into a rate-hike headline is the highest-risk spot in your book right now.

headline: Amazon laying out $1B following Palantir's AI playbook
detail: Big-dollar AI infrastructure spend signals continued enterprise AI buildout, which is a tailwind for chip and semiconductor names like SMH holdings. However, paired with today's Fed warning, AI spend itself is now being cited as an inflation risk - a double-edged signal for your sector.
rec: No action needed today - this supports the long-term SMH and CRWD thesis, but the Fed rate risk offsets any reason to add here. Watch SMH for a clean hold above $600 before considering adding.

headline: Blue Origin pivots after explosion, targeting end of 2026
detail: Relevant context for SPCX (SpaceX), your newest and most volatile position. A competitor stumbling can be read as marginal good news for SpaceX's dominance, but SPCX is a brand-new IPO with very limited data and wide price swings.
rec: No action - SPCX is already in your book. Manage it manually as noted; don't add to a new IPO on margin based on a competitor's setback alone.
